Package com.wip.exception

Class GlobalExceptionHandler

java.lang.Object
com.wip.exception.GlobalExceptionHandler
@RestControllerAdvice(annotations=org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController.class) public class GlobalExceptionHandler extends Object
Centralised exception handler for all REST API controllers annotated with RestController.

This class is annotated with @RestControllerAdvice scoped exclusively to @RestController beans, ensuring that its handlers only intercept exceptions thrown within REST endpoints. UI controllers annotated with @Controller are handled separately by UiExceptionHandler, which renders Thymeleaf HTML error pages instead of JSON payloads.

All handler methods return a ResponseEntity wrapping a structured Map body that contains the following fields:

  • timestamp — the date and time the error occurred
  • status — the numeric HTTP status code
  • error — a short human-readable error type label
  • message — a descriptive message explaining the cause
  • fieldErrors — (validation errors only) a map of field names to their respective constraint violation messages
Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Rohith Varma K

  • Constructor Details

    • GlobalExceptionHandler

      public GlobalExceptionHandler()

  • Method Details

    • handleResourceNotFound

      @ExceptionHandler(ResourceNotFoundException.class) public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Map<String,Object>> handleResourceNotFound(ResourceNotFoundException ex)
      Handles ResourceNotFoundException thrown when a requested entity cannot be found.

      Returns an HTTP 404 Not Found response with a JSON body containing the exception message so that REST clients can display a meaningful error to the end user.

      Parameters:
      ex - the ResourceNotFoundException that was thrown by a service or controller
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with HTTP status 404 and a JSON error body

    • handleValidationErrors

      @ExceptionHandler(org.springframework.web.bind.MethodArgumentNotValidException.class) public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Map<String,Object>> handleValidationErrors(org.springframework.web.bind.MethodArgumentNotValidException ex)
      Handles MethodArgumentNotValidException thrown when a request body fails Bean Validation constraints.

      Iterates over all field-level constraint violations collected in the binding result and builds a fieldErrors map keyed by field name. Returns an HTTP 400 Bad Request response so the REST client can highlight the exact form fields that are invalid.

      Parameters:
      ex - the MethodArgumentNotValidException containing the full binding result with all constraint violations
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with HTTP status 400 and a JSON error body that includes a fieldErrors map of fieldName → errorMessage

    • handleIllegalState

      @ExceptionHandler(java.lang.IllegalStateException.class) public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Map<String,Object>> handleIllegalState(IllegalStateException ex)
      Handles IllegalStateException thrown when a business-rule violation is detected.

      Common scenarios include attempting to create a duplicate record or performing a state transition that is not permitted (e.g., closing an already-closed shipment). Returns an HTTP 400 Bad Request response.

      Parameters:
      ex - the IllegalStateException describing the invalid application state
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with HTTP status 400 and a JSON error body

    • handleGenericException

      @ExceptionHandler(java.lang.Exception.class) public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Map<String,Object>> handleGenericException(Exception ex)
      Handles any unrecognised Exception not matched by a more specific handler.

      Acts as a catch-all to prevent raw stack traces from leaking to REST clients. The exception is printed to standard error for server-side diagnosis, while the client receives a generic 500 Internal Server Error response with a safe, non-revealing message.

      Parameters:
      ex - the unexpected Exception that was not handled by a more specific handler
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with HTTP status 500 and a generic JSON error body
